items.prompt: Which three principles does the chapter treat as core to scalable data management?
choices: Only strong consistency, CAP, and sharding
Only Git commits, pull requests, and code review
Only synthetic data, GANs, and active learning
items.explain: Section 12.1.1 defines elasticity, efficiency, and resilience as the three key principles.

items.prompt: Elasticity means permanently provisioning for peak load so resources never change.
items.explain: Elasticity is expanding and contracting resources with workload, especially useful in the cloud.

items.prompt: Which challenge pair is highlighted for large datasets in Section 12.1.2?
choices: Storage and retrieval difficulty as volume grows
Only font choice and slide layout
items.explain: Storage and retrieval are listed among the main challenges, along with performance and cost.

items.prompt: Name one core component of scalable data management besides storage.
items.rubric: Accept query processing / parallel queries / distributed databases, or data lineage tracking.

items.prompt: Resilience includes techniques such as replication and redundancy so data remains accessible after failures.
items.explain: The chapter ties resilience to high availability, fault tolerance, replication, and redundancy.
